title: Glowing Smoky Mexican Beef Huaraches
description: A smoky and molten Mexican dish featuring feather blade beef, this crowd-pleaser is perfect for any occasion, with anti-inflammatory friendly ingredients to boot.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium-high heat and sear the beef strips until browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the diced onion to the skillet and cook until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for an additional minute.
Add the chipotle peppers to the skillet and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked beef mixture, shredded cheese, chopped cilantro, lime juice, salt, and pepper, and stir until well combined.
To assemble the huaraches, place a portion of the beef mixture onto a corn tortilla and top with another tortilla.
Place the huaraches on a baking sheet and bake in the preheated oven for 10 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and the tortillas are crispy.
Remove the huaraches from the oven and let cool for a few minutes before serving.
Serve the huaraches hot, garnished with additional cilantro and lime wedges if desired.
